qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: SOAP attachment retrieval bypass leaks attachments on private bugnotes
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory fixes improper type handling of the SOAP login password in mci_check_login() within api/soap/mc_api.php on MySQL, while this finding concerns missing private-bugnote attachment authorization in mc_issue_attachment_get()/mci_file_get() and does not involve authentication bypass.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's vulnerable SOAP login path or its MySQL-specific password type-conversion authentication bypass in mci_check_login().
evidence: GHSA-phrq-pc6r-f6gh describes authentication bypass through the SOAP API on MySQL caused by improper type checking of the password parameter.
The upstream patch b349e5c890eeda9bd82e7c7e14479853f8a30d9f modifies only api/soap/mc_api.php and adds ?string typing plus null-to-empty-string normalization in mci_check_login().
The only qualifying finding targets unauthorized attachment reads via mc_issue_attachment_get() and mci_file_get(), where bugnote-level attachment authorization is skipped for private bugnotes.
Because the qualifying finding concerns a distinct attachment ACL flaw rather than the advisory's SOAP authentication logic, it is an open-world finding instead of an advisory match.
